warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose: The Fukui reactivity analysis of ferrocene was performed using the GFN2-xTB method in the gas phase with charge 0 and multiplicity 1. The most electrophilic site (with the largest Fukui-plus function value, indicating preference for nucleophilic attack) is at the iron atom (Fe), with a Fukui index (f+) of 0.22885456. Similarly, Fe is also the most nucleophilic site (according to the Fukui-minus function), with an f- index of 0.25938191. Calculations converged reliably, and the results are considered trustworthy. Warnings reported by the engine include checks on charge conservation for Fukui functions (both f+ and f- sums are ~1.0 as expected).
